name: Prisms

x.formalRole: A composable optic focusing on one case of a sum type: a partial getter (may miss) plus a total constructor (reverse).
x.compositionRule: preview : Prism s a → s → Option a; review : Prism s a → a → s; prisms compose with lenses and other optics.
laws: review then preview round-trips (preview p (review p a) = Some a)
if preview succeeds, re-reviewing reconstructs the source
x.encoding: Optics libraries (Monocle, optics-ts, Haskell lens). In mainstream langs: pattern matching covers many uses.
x.useCases: Focusing/updating a specific variant of a sum type, parsing/printing, and nested optional access.
misuseRisks: Optic-heavy code where pattern matching is clearer

def: The sum-type counterpart of a lens: a composable optic that focuses on one constructor of a variant, with a partial "extract" and a total "build".
problem: Access and update a particular case of a tagged union (and nested ones) compositionally, without manual matching at every layer.
mechanism: A prism pairs preview (s → Option a, succeeds only for the targeted case) with review (a → s, builds the case). Prisms compose with lenses to reach deep into mixed product/sum structures.
benefits: Pairs with lenses for deep nested access
limits: Optics machinery is heavy for simple cases
failures: Overusing optics where match/if-let is clearer
Breaking the round-trip laws in custom prisms
myths: "Lenses are enough" — lenses target product fields; prisms target sum cases
